top_signals.title: How Ukraine Put Russia on the Backfoot
top_signals.summary:
Ukraine’s expanding use of drones at all ranges has changed the war’s momentum. This episode examines how Kyiv is reaching deeper into Russian territory, disrupting operations and logistics as well as energy infrastructure. How is Russia adapting (or not)? Can these strikes produce lasting advantages on the battlefield? Will this force Russia to escalate? Mike Kofman and Dara Massicot join Ryan to discuss.This episode is brought to you by Booz Allen. Learn more at warontherocks.com/boozallen.Image: ArmyInform

The post How Ukraine Put Russia on the Backfoot appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Yen’s Slump in Trade-Weighted Gauge Shows Its Broad-Based Drop
top_signals.summary: While the yen’s plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the currency’s strength is sending an equally worrying signal for Japan.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Tesco Mobile breaches £600m debt facility after reporting failure
top_signals.summary: The UK’s biggest virtual mobile network operator has breached the terms of its debt facility after a reporting shortfall, City AM can reveal. Tesco Mobile, which has more than 5m customers and is owned by Virgin Media O2 and Tesco, identified a breach in covenants in the funding agreement it has with the Royal Bank [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
